> [!NOTE]
> This API is part of a Preview release and is provided for evaluation and development purposes only. It may change based on feedback and is not recommended for production use.

This API supports [pagination](/rest/api/fabric/articles/pagination). A maximum of 10,000 records can be returned per request. With the continuous token provided in the response, you can get the next 10,000 records.

## Permissions

The caller must be a Fabric administrator or authenticate using a service principal.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Tenant.Read.All or Tenant.ReadWrite.All

## Limitations

Maximum 200 requests per hour.

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|

## Interface

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The workspace type. Supported types are personal, workspace, adminworkspace.

parameters.name: capacityId
parameters.description: The capacity ID of the workspace.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The workspace name.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The workspace state. Supported states are active and deleted.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: Continuation token. Used to get the next items in the list.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* BadRequest - Query parameters such as `workspaceType` or `workspaceState` aren't valid.

* InsufficientPrivileges - The caller doesn't have permissions to call the API.

get.summary: Returns a list of active Fabric and PowerBI items.
get.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This API is part of a Preview release and is provided for evaluation and development purposes only. It may change based on feedback and is not recommended for production use.

This API supports [pagination](/rest/api/fabric/articles/pagination). A maximum of 10,000 records can be returned per request. With the continuous token provided in the response, you can get the next 10,000 records.

Page order:
 1. Fabric items
 2. Datamarts
 3. Reports
 4. Dashboards
 5. SemanticModels
 6. Apps
 7. Dataflows

## Permissions

The caller must be a Fabric administrator or authenticate using a service principal.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Tenant.Read.All or Tenant.ReadWrite.All

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

default.description:

* ItemNotFound - Item ID doesn't exist.

* InvalidItemType - Item type isn't valid.

* InvalidItemState - Item state isn't valid.

get.summary: Returns info for all domains (preview).
get.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This preview API will be deprecated on March 31, 2026, and replaced by a stable version, available [here](/rest/api/fabric/admin/domains/list-domains).
The new version introduces breaking changes and is not backward compatible. **When calling this API, callers must specify `true` as the value for the query parameter `preview`.**

## Deprecation Notice

A new query parameter `preview` has been introduced to facilitate this transition:
*   The `preview` query parameter currently defaults to `true`. 
*   Set the value of the `preview` query parameter to `false` to use the Release version of this API.
*   Starting March 31, 2026, the default value for `preview` will change to `false`.

It is recommended to migrate your integration to use the Release version as soon as possible by specifying `false` for the `preview` query parameter (**the default value for the `preview` query parameter will be set to `false` on API's deprecation date**).

The following incompatible changes were introduced in the Release version:
*   Response property `contributorsScope` was removed.

## Permissions

The caller must be a Fabric administrator.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Tenant.Read.All or Tenant.ReadWrite.All.

## Limitations

Maximum 25 requests per one minute per principal.

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: nonEmptyOnly
parameters.description: When true, only return domains that have at least one workspace containing an item. Default: false.

parameters.name: preview
parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which version of the API to use. Set to `true` to use the preview version described on this page, or to `false` to use the Release version detailed [here](/rest/api/fabric/admin/domains/list-domains).
Starting March 31, 2026, the default value for `preview` will change to `false`.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* UnknownError - An error occurred.

post.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This preview API will be deprecated on March 31, 2026, and replaced by a stable version, available [here](/rest/api/fabric/admin/domains/create-domain). The new version introduces breaking changes and is not backward compatible. **When calling this API, callers must specify `true` as the value for the query parameter `preview`.**

## Deprecation Notice

A new query parameter `preview` has been introduced to facilitate this transition:
*   The `preview` query parameter currently defaults to `true`.
*   Set the value of the `preview` query parameter to `false` to use the Release version of this API.
*   Starting March 31, 2026, the default value for `preview` will change to `false`.

It is recommended to migrate your integration to use the Release version as soon as possible by specifying `false` for the `preview` query parameter (**the default value for the `preview` query parameter will be set to `false` on API's deprecation date**).

The following incompatible changes were introduced in the Release version:
*   Response property `contributorsScope` was removed.

## Permissions

## Required Delegated Scopes

Tenant.Read.All or Tenant.ReadWrite.All.

## Limitations

| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: createDomainRequest
parameters.description: The request payload for creating the domain.

parameters.description:
This parameter specifies which version of the API to use. Set to `true` to use the preview version described on this page, or to `false` to use the Release version detailed [here](/rest/api/fabric/admin/domains/create-domain).
Starting March 31, 2026, the default value for `preview` will change to `false`.

* EntityNotFound - Create domain failed because parent domain ID not found.

* EntityConflict - Create domain failed because domain display name already exists.

* InvalidInput - Invalid parent domain ID.

get.summary: Returns the specified domain info (preview).
get.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This preview API will be deprecated on March 31, 2026, and replaced by a stable version, available [here](/rest/api/fabric/admin/domains/get-domain). The new version introduces breaking changes and is not backward compatible. **When calling this API, callers must specify `true` as the value for the query parameter `preview`.**

## Deprecation Notice

A new query parameter `preview` has been introduced to facilitate this transition:
*   The `preview` query parameter currently defaults to `true`.
*   Set the value of the `preview` query parameter to `false` to use the Release version of this API.
*   Starting March 31, 2026, the default value for `preview` will change to `false`.

It is recommended to migrate your integration to use the Release version as soon as possible by specifying `false` for the `preview` query parameter (**the default value for the `preview` query parameter will be set to `false` on API's deprecation date**).

The following incompatible changes were introduced in the Release version:
*   Response property `contributorsScope` was removed.

## Permissions

The caller must be a Fabric administrator.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Tenant.Read.All or Tenant.ReadWrite.All.

## Limitations

Maximum 25 requests per one minute per principal.

post.summary: Remove sensitivity labels from Fabric items (such as lakehouses and reports) by item ID. The sensitivity labels of the autogenerated items linked to the items in the call, are removed and their IDs aren't returned. Items with linked autogenerated items that are supported are: Lakehouse, Warehouse, Datamart, SQLDatabase, MirroredDatabase.
post.description:
For a usage example, see [Set or remove sensitivity labels](/power-bi/admin/service-security-sensitivity-label-inheritance-set-remove-api).

## Permissions

- The user must be a Fabric Administrator.
- The admin user must have sufficient [usage rights](/azure/information-protection/configure-usage-rights) to delete labels.

## Required Scope

Tenant.ReadWrite.All

## Limitations

- Maximum 25 requests per hour.
- Each request can update up to 2,000 Fabric items.

post.summary: Set sensitivity labels on Fabric items, such as lakehouses and reports, by item ID. The sensitivity labels are applied to the autogenerated items related to the requested items, and their IDs aren't returned. Items with linked autogenerated items that are supported are: Lakehouse, Warehouse, Datamart, SQLDatabase and MirroredDatabase.
post.description:
To set a sensitivity label using this API the admin user or the delegated user, if provided, must have the label included in their [label policy](/purview/create-sensitivity-labels?view=o365-worldwide). For a usage example see: [Set or remove sensitivity labels](/power-bi/admin/service-security-sensitivity-label-inheritance-set-remove-api).

## Permissions

- The user must be a Fabric Administrator.
- The admin user and the delegated user, if provided, must have sufficient [usage rights](/azure/information-protection/configure-usage-rights) to set labels.

## Permissions

post.summary: Deletes all organization sharing links for all Fabric items in the tenant. This action cannot be undone.
post.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This API is part of a Preview release and is provided for evaluation and development purposes only. It may change based on feedback and is not recommended for production use.

This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).

Use [LinksSharedToWholeOrganization](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/power-bi/admin) PowerBI Admin REST API to get Power BI Reports shared via organizational sharing links.

## Permissions

The caller must be a Fabric administrator.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Tenant.ReadWrite.All

## Limitations

- Maximum 10 requests per minute.
- Only Power BI Reports are supported.

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: removeAllSharingLinksRequest
parameters.description: Type of sharing links to be removed.

202.description: Request accepted. Retrieving the definition is in progress.

Location.description: The URL of the operation status, which can be used to track the operation state.

x-ms-operation-id.description: The operation ID which can be used with long running operations (LRO) APIs to track the operation state and get the result.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* Forbidden - The caller doesn't have permissions to call the API.

post.summary: Deletes all organization sharing links for the specified Fabric items. This action cannot be undone.
post.description:
> [!NOTE]
> This API is part of a Preview release and is provided for evaluation and development purposes only. It may change based on feedback and is not recommended for production use.

This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).

Use [LinksSharedToWholeOrganization](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/power-bi/admin) PowerBI Admin REST API to get Power BI Reports shared via organizational sharing links.

## Permissions

The caller must be a Fabric administrator.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Tenant.ReadWrite.All

## Limitations

- Maximum 10 requests per minute.
- Each request can delete organization sharing links for up to 500 Fabric items.
- Only Power BI Reports are supported.
